prompt: Cold War propaganda-poster illustration of Strings over a row of ministers, steel-blue duotone on aged paper, mid-century lithograph texture with a halftone screen, no text, no borders, single centered subject, 512×512.

name: Grain Sales to Soviets
note: Grain silos bound for Moscow
prompt: Cold War propaganda-poster illustration of Grain silos bound for Moscow, steel-blue duotone on aged paper, mid-century lithograph texture with a halftone screen, no text, no borders, single centered subject, 512×512.
text: The US randomly selects 1 card from the USSR’s hand (if available). The US must either play the card or return it to the USSR. If the card is returned, or the USSR has no cards, the US may use the Operations value of this card to conduct Operations.

name: John Paul II Elected Pope
note: A pope blesses the Kraków throng
prompt: Cold War propaganda-poster illustration of A pope blesses the Kraków throng, steel-blue duotone on aged paper, mid-century lithograph texture with a halftone screen, no text, no borders, single centered subject, 512×512.
text: Remove 2 USSR Influence from Poland and add 1 US Influence to Poland. This Event allows the “#101 – Solidarity” card to be played as an Event.

name: Latin American Death Squads
note: Headlights on a midnight road
prompt: Cold War propaganda-poster illustration of Headlights on a midnight road, sepia duotone on aged paper, mid-century lithograph texture with a halftone screen, no text, no borders, single centered subject, 512×512.
text: All of the phasing player’s Coup Attempts in Central and South America, for the remainder of this turn, receive +1 to their die roll. All of the opponent’s Coup Attempts in Central and South America, for the remainder of this turn, receive -1 to their die roll.

name: OAS Founded

prompt: Cold War propaganda-poster illustration of Flags of the Americas, ringed, steel-blue duotone on aged paper, mid-century lithograph texture with a halftone screen, no text, no borders, single centered subject, 512×512.

prompt: Cold War propaganda-poster illustration of Nixon’s handshake in Beijing, steel-blue duotone on aged paper, mid-century lithograph texture with a halftone screen, no text, no borders, single centered subject, 512×512.

name: Sadat Expels Soviets

name: “Ask Not What Your Country…”

name: Iran-Iraq War

text: Iran invades Iraq or vice versa (player’s choice). Roll a die and subtract (-1) from the die roll for every enemy controlled country adjacent to the target of the invasion (Iran or Iraq). On a modified die roll of 4-6, the player receives 2 VP and replaces all the opponent’s Influence in the target country with their Influence. The player adds 2 to its Military Operations Track.

name: Defectors
note: A defector slips across the wire
prompt: Cold War propaganda-poster illustration of A defector slips across the wire, steel-blue duotone on aged paper, mid-century lithograph texture with a halftone screen, no text, no borders, single centered subject, 512×512.
text: The US may play this card during the Headline Phase in order to cancel the USSR Headline Event (including a scoring card). The canceled card is placed into the discard pile. If this card is played by the USSR during its action round, the US gains 1 VP.

name: The Cambridge Five

prompt: Cold War propaganda-poster illustration of Five silhouettes, secrets passed west, crimson-red duotone on aged paper, mid-century lithograph texture with a halftone screen, no text, no borders, single centered subject, 512×512.
text: The US reveals all scoring cards in their hand of cards. The USSR player may add 1 USSR Influence to a single Region named on one of the revealed scoring cards. This card can not be played as an Event during the Late War.

name: Special Relationship

name: NORAD
note: A radar sweep over the frozen north
prompt: Cold War propaganda-poster illustration of A radar sweep over the frozen north, steel-blue duotone on aged paper, mid-century lithograph texture with a halftone screen, no text, no borders, single centered subject, 512×512.

name: Che

prompt: Cold War propaganda-poster illustration of Che’s beret against the sierra, crimson-red duotone on aged paper, mid-century lithograph texture with a halftone screen, no text, no borders, single centered subject, 512×512.
text: The USSR may perform a Coup Attempt, using this card’s Operations value, against a non-Battleground country in Central America, South America or Africa. The USSR may perform a second Coup Attempt, against a different non-Battleground country in Central America, South America or Africa, if the first Coup Attempt removed any US Influence from the target country.

name: Our Man in Tehran

prompt: Cold War propaganda-poster illustration of An agent in the Tehran bazaar, steel-blue duotone on aged paper, mid-century lithograph texture with a halftone screen, no text, no borders, single centered subject, 512×512.

The US may discard any or all of the drawn cards, after revealing the discarded card(s) to the USSR player, without triggering the Event(s). Any remaining drawn cards are returned to the draw pile and the draw pile is reshuffled.

name: Yuri and Samantha
